2603.15724.title: Meta-TTRL: A Metacognitive Framework for Self-Improving Test-Time Reinforcement Learning in Unified Multimodal Models
2603.15724.paper_summary: Existing test-time scaling (TTS) methods for unified multimodal models (UMMs) in text-to-image (T2I) generation primarily rely on search or sampling strategies that produce only instance-level improvements, limiting the ability to learn from prior inferences and accumulate knowledge across similar prompts. To overcome these limitations, we propose Meta-TTRL, a metacognitive test-time reinforcement learning framework. Meta-TTRL performs test-time parameter optimization guided by model-intrinsic monitoring signals derived from the meta-knowledge of UMMs, achieving self-improvement and capability-level improvement at test time. Extensive experiments demonstrate that Meta-TTRL generalizes well across three representative UMMs, including Janus-Pro-7B, BAGEL, and Qwen-Image, achieving significant gains on compositional reasoning tasks and multiple T2I benchmarks with limited data. We provide the first comprehensive analysis to investigate the potential of test-time reinforcement learning (TTRL) for T2I generation in UMMs. Our analysis further reveals a key insight underlying effective TTRL: metacognitive synergy, where monitoring signals align with the model's optimization regime to enable self-improvement.
2603.15724.paper_summary_zh: 現有的統一多模態模型（UMMs）在文本到圖像（T2I）生成中的測試時間擴展（TTS）方法主要依賴於搜索或取樣策略，這些策略僅能產生實例級別的改進，限制了從先前推斷中學習和在相似提示之間積累知識的能力。為了克服這些限制，我們提出了Meta-TTRL，一種元認知測試時間增強學習框架。Meta-TTRL在測試時間執行參數優化，這是由來自UMMs的元知識衍生的模型內在監控信號指導的，實現了自我改進和能力級別的提升。大量實驗表明，Meta-TTRL在三個代表性的UMMs上具有良好的泛化能力，包括Janus-Pro-7B、BAGEL和Qwen-Image，在組合推理任務和多個T2I基準測試中取得了顯著的增益，且數據量有限。我們提供了首個全面分析，以探討測試時間增強學習（TTRL）在UMMs中對T2I生成的潛力。我們的分析進一步揭示了有效TTRL的關鍵見解：元認知協同，其中監控信號與模型的優化機制對齊，以實現自我改進。
2603.15724.author: Lit Sin Tan et.al.
2603.15724.authors: Lit Sin Tan, Junzhe Chen, Xiaolong Fu, Lichen Ma, Junshi Huang, Jianzhong Shi, Yan Li, Lijie Wen
